name: businesses
title: Commerce Business API
description: This API is capable of creating, updating, deleting and fetching businesses based on parameters such as a businessId and attributeName.

endpoints.description: This endpoint can be used to obtain a list of all the available bussinesses for a specific organization.

parameters.name: includeFields
parameters.description: This is the list of fields that will be included in the response

parameters.name: If-Modified-Since
parameters.description: Check if the data has been modified since a given date

schema.title: Store List
schema.description: Resource representing a list of Businesses

items.description: Array containing the current page of Businesses

items.title: Business
items.description: Business resource represents a merchant business

activeSince.description: This is a response only field. It is set internally and returned in response.  The time (in ISO-8601 format) at which the first terminal at the business was activated. E.g. 2014-09-11T23:14:44Z.

addressLine1.description: The first line of the address. For example, number and street name. For example, `3032 Bunker Hill Lane`. Required for compliance and risk checks. Must contain the full address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the address. For example, office suite or apartment number.

addressLine3.description: The third line of the address, if needed. For example, a street complement for Brazil; direction text, such as `next to Opera House`; or a landmark reference in an Indian address.

adminArea4.description: The neighborhood, ward, or district. Smaller than `adminArea3` or `subLocality`. For example, the postal sorting code that is used in Guernsey and many French territories, such as French Guiana; the fine-grained administrative levels in China.

adminArea3.description: A sub-locality, suburb, neighborhood, or district. Smaller than `adminArea2`. For example, in Brazil - Suburb, bairro, or neighborhood; in India - Sub-locality or district. Street name information may not always available and a sub-locality or district can reference a very small area.

adminArea2.description: A city, town, or village. Smaller than `adminArea1`.

adminArea1.description: The highest level sub-division in a country, which is usually a province, state, or ISO-3166-2 subdivision; formatted for postal delivery. For example, `CA` and not `California`. For example, for UK - A county; for USs - A state; for Canada - A province; for Japan - A prefecture; for Switzerland - A kanton.

postalCode.description: The postal code, which is the zip code or equivalent. Typically required for countries that have a postal code or an equivalent. See [Postal code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Postal_code).

countryCode.description: The [two-character ISO 3166-1 code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) that identifies the country or region. Note: The country code for Great Britain is `GB` and not `UK` as used in the top-level domain names for that country. Use country code `C2` for China for comparable uncontrolled price (CUP) method, bank-card, and cross-border transactions.

addressDetails.title: Address Details
addressDetails.description: The non-portable additional address details that are sometimes needed for compliance, risk, or other scenarios where fine-grain address information might be needed. Not portable with common third-party and open-source address libraries and redundant with core fields. For example, `address.addressLine1` is usually a combination of `addressDetails.streetNumber` and `streetName` and `streetType`.

streetName.description: The street name. Just `Drury` in `Drury Lane`.

streetType.description: The street type. For example, avenue, boulevard, road, or expressway.

deliveryService.description: The delivery service. Post office box, bag number, or post office name.

buildingName.description: A named locations that represents the premise. Usually a building name or number or collection of buildings with a common name or number. For example, Craven House.

subBuilding.description: The first-order entity below a named building or location that represents the sub-premise. Usually a single building within a collection of buildings with a common name. Can be a flat, story, floor, room, or apartment.

addressType.description: The type of address. Single character representation of a type. For example: 'B' for building, 'F' for organization, 'G' for general delivery, 'H' for high-rise, 'L' for large-volume organization, 'P' for Post Office box or delivery service, 'R' for rural route, 'S' for street, 'U' for unidentified address.

geoCoordinates.description: The latitude and longitude of the address. For example, `37.42242` and `-122.08585`.
geoCoordinates.title: Geographic Coordinates

attributes.description: A name/value pair list that could be persisted and later retreived.
attributes.title: Dictionary

businessUrl.description: The url of the business' website.

closedAt.description: Details on when the business was closed.

closingDetails.description: Details on why the business was closed.

closingReason.description: The reason why the business was closed.

description.description: A short description of the business.

doingBusinessAs.description: The name the business likes to be known as.

domicileCountry.description: A two-character ISO 3166-1 code that identifies the country or region.

industryType.description: The industry type that this business belongs to. (Retail, Restaurant, Lodging, MOTO etc.)

legalName.description: The legal name of the business.

mcc.description: The merchant category code of the business in ISO 18245:2023 format (Refer https://www.citibank.com/tts/solutions/commercial-cards/assets/docs/govt/Merchant-Category-Codes.pdf)

countryCode.description: The country calling code (CC), in its canonical international [E.164 numbering plan format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en). The combined length of the CC and the national number must not be greater than 15 digits. The national number consists of a national destination code (NDC) and subscriber number (SN).

nationalNumber.description: The national number, in its canonical international [E.164 numbering plan format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en). The combined length of the country calling code (CC) and the national number must not be greater than 15 digits. The national number consists of a national destination code (NDC) and subscriber number (SN).

scrubbed.description: Indicates if business information is scrubbed to protect privacy of the merchant.

sic.description: The standard industry code of the business. (https://siccode.com/sic-code-lookup-directory)

status.description: It is set to ADDED as soon as the business is added into the system. Moved to ACTIVATED once the first terminal is activated. It may move to LOCKED if for whatever reason, operations need to be stopped. It is moved to CLOSED if the business is closed for good.

subscribedBundles.description: The list of bundles this business has subscribed to.

items.title: Bundled Features

createdAt.description: This is a response only field. The time (in ISO-8601 format) at which the bundle was created. E.g. 2014-09-11T23:14:44Z.

featureConfig.description: Optional configs for each feature in the list.
featureConfig.title: Dictionary

featureList.description: List of features the bundle contains.

updatedAt.description: This is a response only field. The time (in ISO-8601 format) at which the bundle was updated. E.g. 2014-09-11T23:14:44Z.

items.description: This is the base object for the list of features a business/store has subscribed to.

timezone.description: The timezone that the headquarters belong to.

type.description: The type of business. Use TEST_MERCHANT to create a business for testing purpose, and MERCHANT otherwise.

updatedAt.description: Last updated time of the business.

paymentDetails.description: Contains payment details used by the business.
paymentDetails.title: Business Payment Details

acquirer.description: The merchant acquirer to facilitate card payments for this business. This value is required.
acquirer.title: Business Payment Processor

externalMerchantId.description: This is the unique identifier assigned by the acquirer to the business. It is a business level MID.

processor.description: The payment processor to facilitate payments for this business.
processor.title: Business Payment Processor

processorData.description: The processor related data to be used by the business.
processorData.title: Dictionary

underwritingLevel.description: The application level of the business for underwriting.

totalItems.description: Total number of Businesses matching the search criteria. Returned only when totalRequired is set to true.

totalPages.description: Total number of pages matching the search criteria. Returned only when totalRequired is set to true.

links.description: Array of HATEOAS link relations that should be used to navigate across pages of Businesses

schema.title: Business
schema.description: Business resource represents a merchant business

activeSince.description: This is a response only field. It is set internally and returned in response.  The time (in ISO-8601 format) at which the first terminal at the business was activated. E.g. 2014-09-11T23:14:44Z.

addressLine1.description: The first line of the address. For example, number and street name. For example, `3032 Bunker Hill Lane`. Required for compliance and risk checks. Must contain the full address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the address. For example, office suite or apartment number.

addressLine3.description: The third line of the address, if needed. For example, a street complement for Brazil; direction text, such as `next to Opera House`; or a landmark reference in an Indian address.

adminArea4.description: The neighborhood, ward, or district. Smaller than `adminArea3` or `subLocality`. For example, the postal sorting code that is used in Guernsey and many French territories, such as French Guiana; the fine-grained administrative levels in China.

adminArea3.description: A sub-locality, suburb, neighborhood, or district. Smaller than `adminArea2`. For example, in Brazil - Suburb, bairro, or neighborhood; in India - Sub-locality or district. Street name information may not always available and a sub-locality or district can reference a very small area.

adminArea2.description: A city, town, or village. Smaller than `adminArea1`.

adminArea1.description: The highest level sub-division in a country, which is usually a province, state, or ISO-3166-2 subdivision; formatted for postal delivery. For example, `CA` and not `California`. For example, for UK - A county; for USs - A state; for Canada - A province; for Japan - A prefecture; for Switzerland - A kanton.

postalCode.description: The postal code, which is the zip code or equivalent. Typically required for countries that have a postal code or an equivalent. See [Postal code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Postal_code).

countryCode.description: The [two-character ISO 3166-1 code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) that identifies the country or region. Note: The country code for Great Britain is `GB` and not `UK` as used in the top-level domain names for that country. Use country code `C2` for China for comparable uncontrolled price (CUP) method, bank-card, and cross-border transactions.

addressDetails.title: Address Details
addressDetails.description: The non-portable additional address details that are sometimes needed for compliance, risk, or other scenarios where fine-grain address information might be needed. Not portable with common third-party and open-source address libraries and redundant with core fields. For example, `address.addressLine1` is usually a combination of `addressDetails.streetNumber` and `streetName` and `streetType`.

streetName.description: The street name. Just `Drury` in `Drury Lane`.

streetType.description: The street type. For example, avenue, boulevard, road, or expressway.

deliveryService.description: The delivery service. Post office box, bag number, or post office name.

buildingName.description: A named locations that represents the premise. Usually a building name or number or collection of buildings with a common name or number. For example, Craven House.

subBuilding.description: The first-order entity below a named building or location that represents the sub-premise. Usually a single building within a collection of buildings with a common name. Can be a flat, story, floor, room, or apartment.

addressType.description: The type of address. Single character representation of a type. For example: 'B' for building, 'F' for organization, 'G' for general delivery, 'H' for high-rise, 'L' for large-volume organization, 'P' for Post Office box or delivery service, 'R' for rural route, 'S' for street, 'U' for unidentified address.

geoCoordinates.description: The latitude and longitude of the address. For example, `37.42242` and `-122.08585`.
geoCoordinates.title: Geographic Coordinates

attributes.description: A name/value pair list that could be persisted and later retreived.
attributes.title: Dictionary

businessUrl.description: The url of the business' website.

closedAt.description: Details on when the business was closed.

closingDetails.description: Details on why the business was closed.

closingReason.description: The reason why the business was closed.

description.description: A short description of the business.

doingBusinessAs.description: The name the business likes to be known as.

domicileCountry.description: A two-character ISO 3166-1 code that identifies the country or region.

industryType.description: The industry type that this business belongs to. (Retail, Restaurant, Lodging, MOTO etc.)

legalName.description: The legal name of the business.

mcc.description: The merchant category code of the business in ISO 18245:2023 format (Refer https://www.citibank.com/tts/solutions/commercial-cards/assets/docs/govt/Merchant-Category-Codes.pdf)

countryCode.description: The country calling code (CC), in its canonical international [E.164 numbering plan format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en). The combined length of the CC and the national number must not be greater than 15 digits. The national number consists of a national destination code (NDC) and subscriber number (SN).

nationalNumber.description: The national number, in its canonical international [E.164 numbering plan format](https://www.itu.int/rec/T-REC-E.164/en). The combined length of the country calling code (CC) and the national number must not be greater than 15 digits. The national number consists of a national destination code (NDC) and subscriber number (SN).

scrubbed.description: Indicates if business information is scrubbed to protect privacy of the merchant.

sic.description: The standard industry code of the business. (https://siccode.com/sic-code-lookup-directory)

status.description: It is set to ADDED as soon as the business is added into the system. Moved to ACTIVATED once the first terminal is activated. It may move to LOCKED if for whatever reason, operations need to be stopped. It is moved to CLOSED if the business is closed for good.

subscribedBundles.description: The list of bundles this business has subscribed to.
